EnterpriseSDK
Table of Contents

Interface IRow

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members that return information about the row, the table the row belongs to and storing and deleting the row.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("FCB01CB4-9F0B-11D0-BEC7-00805F7C4268")]
public interface IRow : IRowBuffer

Property Value

IFields

HasOID

Indicates if the row has an OID.

bool HasOID { get; }

Property Value

bool

OID

The OID for the row.

long OID { get; }

Examples

Sample Snippet

Table

The Table for the row.

ITable Table { get; }

Property Value

ITable

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>A <STRONG>Table </STRONG>object has one or more columns, referred to as fields, and contains an unordered collection of rows. Each row has for each field exactly one value in the data type of the field.</P> <P>A <STRONG>Table </STRONG>is a <STRONG>Dataset </STRONG>and properties such as the name of the table, the persistable name object for the table, and the workspace containing the table, may be obtained via the <STRONG>IDataset </STRONG>interface. In relational terms, a <STRONG>Table </STRONG>object represents an RDBMS table or view. In objected-oriented terms, a Table object represents an ObjectClass or a RelationshipClass in a geodatabase. A <STRONG>Table </STRONG>object hands out <STRONG>Row </STRONG>objects which themselves support application callable methods depending on the type of data stored in the table.</P> <P>The <STRONG>Name </STRONG>property of a <STRONG>Table</STRONG>, accessible via the <STRONG>IDataset </STRONG>interface, returns its fully qualified name. The level of qualification may vary depending on the host DBMS. For example, a table named ‘pipes’, owned by a user named ‘gas’ may be called ‘pipes’ in and Access or File Geodatabase, ‘gas.pipes’ on Oracle and ‘mydb.gas.pipes’ on SQLServer. The <STRONG>ParseTableNamemethod </STRONG>on the <STRONG>ISqlSyntax </STRONG>interface supported by the table’s workspace can be used to split the fully qualified name into its components.</P>

Value[int] 

object Value[int Index] { get; set; }

Index int

object
